typethis 0.7.0

Export typed models or typed functions to OpenAPI 3.1

Source: R/openapi.R
to_openapi.Rd

Builds an OpenAPI 3.1 document fragment from typed models, typed functions, or a mixed list. Schemas are produced via to_json_schema() and lifted into components.schemas; $ref strings are rewritten from the JSON Schema convention (#/$defs/X) to the OpenAPI convention (#/components/schemas/X). Typed functions become a single paths entry with a JSON requestBody carrying the arguments and a 200 response carrying the return type.

Usage

to_openapi(x, info = NULL, paths = NULL, ...)

Arguments

x

A model class name (character scalar), a model constructor or instance, a typed function, or a list mixing any of the above.

info

Optional named list with OpenAPI info fields (title, version, description, ...). Sensible defaults are filled in.

paths

Optional named list of additional paths entries to merge in (e.g. for typed functions added by name).

...

Forwarded to method dispatch.

Value

A list ready for yaml::write_yaml() or jsonlite::toJSON().

Details

To control the path and operation ID for a typed function, set attr(fn, "openapi_op_id") <- "yourId" before passing it in.

See also

write_openapi() for the file-IO convenience wrapper; from_openapi() for the reverse direction; to_json_schema() for the underlying schema export.

Other OpenAPI: from_openapi(), openapi, read_openapi(), write_openapi()

Examples

define_model("User", fields = list(
  id   = field("integer", primary_key = TRUE),
  name = field("character")
))
doc <- to_openapi("User",
  info = list(title = "Users API", version = "1.0.0")
)
names(doc)
#> [1] "openapi"    "info"       "components"
names(doc$components$schemas)
#> [1] "User"
